title: Health & the Body
description: Talk about illness, symptoms, and medical visits — essential vocabulary for real-life situations.

items.example: I have a terrible headache — do you have any painkillers?

items.front: to have a cold / the flu
items.example: She has a cold and can't stop sneezing.
His temperature is 39°C — he has a high fever.
I've been coughing all day. I think I'm getting sick.

items.front: to see a doctor / go to hospital
items.back: ir al médico / ir al hospital
items.example: If it gets worse, you should see a doctor.
The doctor gave me a prescription for antibiotics.
It took me a week to fully recover from the flu.
I'm allergic to nuts — they make me break out in hives.

items.front: to sprain an ankle / break a bone
items.back: torcerse el tobillo / romperse un hueso
items.example: She sprained her ankle during the game and had to leave.

items.prompt: I have a terrible ___. My head is pounding and I can't concentrate.
items.explanation: 'Headache' = pain in the head. 'My head is pounding' confirms this symptom.

items.prompt: The doctor examined me and wrote me a ___ for antibiotics.
items.explanation: A 'prescription' is the written note from a doctor authorising medication.

items.prompt: She has a high ___. Her forehead is really hot.
items.explanation: 'Fever' = elevated body temperature. A hot forehead is a classic symptom.

items.prompt: He fell off his bike and ___ his wrist. He's in a cast now.
items.explanation: 'Broke' = fractured a bone. A cast confirms the bone was broken.

items.prompt: What's the matter? — I don't ___ very well. I think I'm coming down with something.
items.explanation: 'Feel' is the standard verb for describing how you physically or emotionally feel.

items.sentence: I have a terrible ___. Can I have some painkillers?
She has a high ___. She should stay in bed.
The doctor told me to ___ plenty of water and get some rest.
I'm ___ to penicillin — the doctor has to prescribe something else.
He ___ his ankle playing football and had to go to A&E.
